issues.suggested_fix: Use one main h1 for the page purpose, then organize sections with h2 and lower headings.

rule.title: Page has multiple h1 headings
rule.why_it_matters: Several h1 headings make it harder for students to identify the single main topic of the page.
rule.manual_review_notes: Some layouts intentionally use sectioned h1 elements; judge readability.
rule.static_check_limitations: Static HTML cannot evaluate how the headings are presented visually.

issues.message: Fixed or wide content may prevent reflow

evidence.text: Skip to main content An official website of the United States government Here's
evidence.reason: A rendered element is wider than the narrow viewport or uses a large fixed pixel width.

issues.suggested_fix: Replace fixed-width layout with responsive sizing such as max-width: 100%.

rule.title: Fixed or wide content may prevent reflow
rule.why_it_matters: Large fixed-width elements can force horizontal scrolling and hide content when users zoom or use narrow windows.
rule.manual_review_notes: Confirm whether the wide element is essential and whether all content remains reachable without two-dimensional scrolling.
rule.browser_check_limitations: The check flags obvious wide rendered elements and fixed pixel widths; it may miss complex layout problems.

limitations: This prototype runs static HTML checks plus a basic keyboard interaction audit.
It does not replace a full human accessibility audit.
Browser mode does not simulate a full screen reader, and accessible names are estimated.
Low-vision browser checks are conservative heuristics and require manual review.

limitations: Low-vision checks use browser-computed styles and conservative heuristics.
Rendered color contrast checks do not prove full WCAG conformance.
Zoom/reflow uses a narrow viewport approximation rather than a full zoom compliance test.
Focus indicator detection may miss custom focus styles.

ai_scout.summary: Possible accessibility barriers were identified on the U.S. Access Board contact page.

ai_suggested_observations.observation: The page may have multiple h1 headings, which could affect the structure and readability of the content for some users.
ai_suggested_observations.why_it_may_matter: This could potentially cause confusion for users relying on headings to navigate the page, especially those using assistive technologies.
ai_suggested_observations.related_deterministic_evidence: Multiple h1 headings were detected in the page's HTML structure.

ai_suggested_observations.observation: Some content on the page may not reflow properly when the viewport is narrowed, potentially causing issues for users with low vision or those who prefer a narrower viewport.
ai_suggested_observations.why_it_may_matter: This could lead to difficulties in reading or interacting with the content for users who rely on zooming or adjusting the viewport width.
ai_suggested_observations.related_deterministic_evidence: Elements with fixed widths were detected in the page's layout, which may prevent proper reflow.

outreach_notes: We suggest reviewing the page's structure and layout to ensure that they are accessible and usable for all users, including those with disabilities.
limitations: AI Scout findings are suggestions and need human review.
AI Scout output is not a confirmed accessibility finding.
AI Scout does not determine legal compliance, WCAG compliance, or screen-reader behavior.
AI Scout output is not a needs human review accessibility finding.
